medications.indication_rationale: Do not start vasoactive drugs over telehealth before assessment; they should be specialist-guided after diagnosis.
Waist-high compression reduces venous pooling and is first-line for neuropathic POTS; provide/prescribe and advise applying before standing.
Sensible salt/fluid loading supports blood volume; advise within reasonable limits and check no contraindication.
medications.dose_route_frequency: increased fluids and dietary salt within sensible limits

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Fall-safety, shower chair and energy conservation.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Mainstay non-drug measures for neuropathic POTS pooling.
Not an evidence-based remote intervention here.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply waist-high compression before standing, rise slowly with counter-manoeuvres, use a shower chair, and watch for a single pale/cold/numb leg, true fainting, or chest pain (call 000 if they occur).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces pooling/falls and safety-nets limb/cardiac emergencies.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Drink salty electrolyte fluid before getting up and keep fluids/salt sensibly increased.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Expands blood volume to offset pooling.

patient_education_points.point: When you stand, your leg veins aren't tightening, so blood pools and your legs go purple and your brain doesn't get enough blood - that's why you feel faint. It goes normal instantly when you lie down. This fits your neuropathic POTS.
Because both legs behave the same and go back to normal when you elevate them, this is against a clot - but we still want your legs and heart checked in person, so please arrange an urgent review.
Waist-high compression, salty fluids before standing, and rising slowly with leg/core clenches can help now; we won't start vein medications over a chat before you're assessed.
Call 000 if one leg suddenly becomes white, freezing cold and numb, you actually faint, or you get chest pain.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent in-person GP review within 1-2 days (legs + ECG); dysautonomia review afterwards
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Leg examination/pulses, ECG, compression/salt-fluid plan, fall-safety, medication need
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: A single pale/cold/pulseless leg, true syncope or chest pain - call 000.

must_include_items: Screen limb-ischaemia/DVT (single pale/cold/pulseless leg, fixed unilateral calf features) and cardiac features
Advise compression, salt/fluids and counter-manoeuvres; do NOT start vasoactives over chat
Arrange urgent in-person leg + ECG review and manage fall risk
Safety-net a single ischaemic limb, true syncope and chest pain (000)
acceptable_alternatives: Same-day ED if any red-flag feature is present
errors_of_commission: Reflexive ambulance for bilateral elevation-resolving pooling
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) limb-ischaemia/DVT + cardiac screening, (2) supportive compression/salt-fluids without remote vasoactives, and (3) urgent in-person leg/ECG review plus fall-safety. Missing a possible ischaemic limb OR reflexively over-escalating pooling are the failure modes.
